speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: So, your opinion is, Aufidius,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: That they of Rome are entered in our counsels

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: And know how we proceed.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Is it not yours?

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Whatever have been thought on in this state

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: That could be brought to bodily act ere Rome

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Had circumvention? ’Tis not four days gone

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Since I heard thence. These are the words—I think

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I have the letter here. Yes, here it is.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: They have pressed a power, but it is not known

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Whether for east or west. The dearth is great.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: The people mutinous; and, it is rumored,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Cominius, Martius your old enemy,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Who is of Rome worse hated than of you,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And Titus Lartius, a most valiant Roman,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: These three lead on this preparation

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Whither ’tis bent. Most likely ’tis for you.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Consider of it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Our army’s in the field.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: We never yet made doubt but Rome was ready

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: To answer us.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Nor did you think it folly

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: To keep your great pretenses veiled till when

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: They needs must show themselves, which, in the hatching,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: It seemed, appeared to Rome. By the discovery

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: We shall be shortened in our aim, which was

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: To take in many towns ere almost Rome

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Should know we were afoot.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: Noble Aufidius,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: Take your commission; hie you to your bands.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: Let us alone to guard Corioles.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: If they set down before ’s, for the remove

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: Bring up your army. But I think you’ll find

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: They’ve not prepared for us.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: O, doubt not that;

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I speak from certainties. Nay, more,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Some parcels of their power are forth already,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And only hitherward. I leave your Honors.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: If we and Caius Martius chance to meet,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: ’Tis sworn between us we shall ever strike

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Till one can do no more.

speaker: ALL
text: The gods assist you!

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And keep your Honors safe!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Farewell.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SENATOR
text: Farewell.

speaker: ALL
text: Farewell.
